other_info:
Publications
Deep Neural Network Augmented Wireless Channel Estimation for Preamble-based OFDM PHY on Zynq System on Chip

IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems · May 6, 2023

Show publication
Reliable and fast channel estimation is crucial for next-generation wireless networks supporting a wide range of vehicular and low-latency services. Recently, deep learning (DL) based channel estimation has been explored as an efficient alternative to conventional least-square (LS) and linear minimum mean square error (LMMSE) approaches. Most of these DL approaches have not been realized on system-on-chip (SoC), and preliminary study shows that their complexity exceeds the complexity of the entire physical layer (PHY). The high latency of DL is another concern. This paper considers the design and implementation of deep neural network (DNN) augmented LS-based channel estimation (LSDNN) for preamble-based orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) physical layer (PHY) on SoC. We demonstrate the gain in performance compared to the conventional LS and LMMSE approaches. Via software-hardware co-design, word-length optimization, and reconfigurable architectures, we demonstrate the superiority of the LSDNN over the LS and LMMSE for a wide range of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), number of pilots, preamble types, and wireless channels. Further, we evaluate the performance, power, and area (PPA) of the LS and LSDNN application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) implementations in 45 nm technology. We demonstrate that word-length optimization can substantially improve PPA for the proposed architecture in ASIC implementations.

projects.raw_text:
Improving accuracy of Peak Power Estimation of Digital Circuits using ATPG Based Techniques [MTech. Capstone Project]

Jul 2021 – May 2022

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
This work proposes an ATPG-based technique that delivers higher peak power estimates than traditional techniques and helps verify a circuit for the worst case. We use Synopsys Tetramax to obtain test patterns that toggle heuristically selected target nets followed by intelligent pattern compaction and test stimuli generation. We use a logic simulator to generate a Switching Activity Interchange Format (SAIF) file and measure dynamic power dissipation. We compare the estimates of power dissipation of the proposed methodology with the power estimates obtained using test-benches that attained a high coverage by employing constrained random verification methodology. The results demonstrate the proposed methodology is superior to methods based on comprehensive functional coverage for peak power estimation.

Skills: RTL Power estimation 

Other contributors

View all contributors
In Memory Compute for GPUs

Jan 2022 – May 2022

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
In-memory compute logic is designed for GPUs using 8T_SRAM. The operations implemented in-memory are: NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR, IMPLY, Add, Subtract, Compare.
The In-memory compute based design gives atleast 33% better performance in write and compute mode and 100% better performance in compute only mode, when compare with non in-memory design.
CUDA is used to benchmark performance for NVidia GPUs.

Other contributors



View all contributors
Deep Neural Network Augmented Wireless Channel Estimation on System on Chip

Jan 2022 – Apr 2022


Associated with ECE Department IIIT Delhi

Show project
In this work, we consider the design and implementation of deep neural network (DNN) augmented LS-based channel estimation on Zynq multi-processor SoC (ZMPSoC). 
Further, we took the design through the ASIC flow to perform timing sign-off at its best-operating frequency and compare the PPA of LS and DNN augmented LS,  followed by analyzing the benefits in PPA upon word-length optimization.

Other contributors



View all contributors
A gm/Id based approach to design High Gain Folded Cascode Amplifier

Aug 2021 – Dec 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
Design and comparison of High Gain Folded Cascode Operational Amplifier, using the Classical "Square law" and "gm/Id technique" on Cadence Virtuoso, by modelling the aspect ratio of different transistors in the circuit in "Square law approach" and identifying operating regions of different transistors by modelling "gm/Id" of different transistors in the gm/Id based technique. The corresponding different curves with respect to gm/Id plots were extracted in Cadence Virtuoso using Ocean-scripts in ADEL.

Skills: Analog Integrated Circuit Design

Other contributors

View all contributors
Design and benchmark of Pulsed Latch

Aug 2021 – Dec 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
We worked on improving the power and performance (speed) of a latch by designing the Pulsed Latch, where we modelled various parameters of the schematic in Cadence Virtuoso to meet the required specifications (Set-up & Hold requirements) of Pre layout simulations in Adel and Eldo. Further, we proceeded with the DRC and LVS clean layout of the circuit, which was optimally optimized wrt area as much as possible. Finally, we verified the specifications by doing post layout Eldo simulations and plotting the necessary outputs on the EZ wave.

Skills: Digital VLSI Design

Other contributors



View all contributors
Designing an Emulated System on Chip (SoC) using OOPs in Python

Aug 2021 – Nov 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
INHERITANCE, POLYMORPHISM, ENCAPSULATION, ABSTRACTION, FILE HANDLING, and EXCEPTION HANDLING are the OOPS Principles deployed in SOC Design using Python.

Skills: Programming -- C, C++, Python & Matlab

Other contributors




View all contributors
Modeling Functional Coverage using Constrained Randomization in System Verilog

Sep 2021 – Oct 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
While Functional Coverage is one of the most important metrics, giving an objective measure of the design ‘intent’ being effectively exercised by the applied stimulus in the Verification industry, this analysis of modelling Functional Coverage of test stimuli using the constraint randomization feature of System Verilog opens another perspective toward the usage of “Functional Coverage” as an important metric to benchmark various input-dependent design parameters.

Skills: RTL Power estimation , Verification -- Verilog, System Verilog & UVM

Other contributors

View all contributors
Implementation and Optimization of Fog Rectification Algorithm in SDSOC

Jan 2021 – May 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
In this project, we learned a lot about Image processing,  where we proceeded for fog elimination by performing dark channel estimation, followed by applying various filter operations like bilateral and minimum filtering on the input image matrix, and finally contrast enhancement of the obtained image was done using histogram equalization.Initially, the whole algorithm was executed in C language on software in SDSOC. Further, we proceeded with the hardware-software partitioning of our algorithm,  to observe for various bottlenecks in our code and the required portion of our code was moved in hardware, in order to optimize the whole task for power, performance and resource utilization.

Other contributors




View all contributors
Negative Bitline Write Driver Circuit Design

Jan 2021 – May 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
We worked on improving the writability of SRAM memory cell by designing the Negative Bitline Write Assist circuit, where we modelled various parameters of the schematic in Cadence Virtuoso to meet the required specifications of Pre layout simulations in Adel and Eldo. Further, we proceeded with the DRC and LVS clean layout of the circuit, which was optimally optimized wrt area as much as possible. Finally,  we verified the specifications by doing post layout eldo simulations and plotting the necessary outputs on ez wave.

Skills: Digital VLSI Design

Other contributors

View all contributors
VLSI Design Flow (RTL to GDS) of a Finite State Machine on Cadence design tools

Jan 2021 – Apr 2021

Associated with IIIT Delhi

Show project
RTL to GDS Flow involving RTL Verilog Coding, simulations and code coverage analysis, logical synthesis, Formal Equivalence Checking, STA, Test Insertion and Physical Design.

Skills: ASIC Design Flow (RTL to GDS)

Other contributors



View all contributors
Open Sourced Brain Computer Interface using EEG [BTech. Final Year Project]

Jul 2019 – Jul 2020

Associated with National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT)

Show project
Our final year project is detection of brain waves using an EEG headset and creating an IOT interface that will enable the end users to control various operations with their mind with simple programming.

Other contributors

View all contributors
"Obstacle Avoiding Robot" interfacing "Ultrasonic sensor" and "Motor Shield" to "Arduino".

Apr 2017 – May 2017

Associated with National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT)

Show project
The project uses Ultrasonic sensor "HC SR04" to detect objects in the surroundings and sends it to the "Arduino Uno" micro controller board , based on which it's movements are directed and controlled with the help of motor shield "Ada fruit" , such that it can move in any unknown environments without colliding with other objects.
